Rishikesh is a holy town on the Ganges at the foothills of the Himalayas, known as the Yoga Capital of the World, famous for ashrams, temples, river-rafting, spiritual retreats and natural beauty.
Alappuzha (Alleppey) is known for its serene backwaters, houseboat cruises, canals, beaches and lagoons; often called the 'Venice of the East'.
Gulmarg is a picturesque hill station in the Himalayas, famous for skiing in winter, scenic meadows, gondola rides (one of the highest in the world), and natural beauty.

Ladakh (Leh) is a high-altitude desert mountain region nestled in the Himalayas, known for its rugged scenic landscapes, Buddhist monasteries, dramatic valleys (Nubra, Zanskar), turquoise lakes (Pangong), high passes, and unique mountain-culture
Hampi is a legendary historical town on the banks of the Tungabhadra River — once the thriving capital of the Vijayanagara Empire

Coorg, also known as Kodagu, is a popular hill station in Karnataka, famous for coffee plantations, lush forests, waterfalls, wildlife sanctuaries, and cultural heritage. Major attractions include Abbey Falls, Raja’s Seat, Dubare Elephant Camp, and Talakaveri.

Araku Valley is a picturesque hill station in the Eastern Ghats, famous for coffee plantations, waterfalls, tribal culture, and scenic train rides through lush forests and valleys. Major attractions include Katiki Waterfalls, Borra Caves, and Ananthagiri Hills.
